edges.description: Adventitial inflammation promotes Rho-kinase upregulation in coronary vascular smooth muscle cells, increasing hypercontractility.
VSMC hypercontraction is reinforced by impaired endothelial vasodilator signaling and abnormal eNOS activity.
Episodic coronary narrowing lowers coronary blood flow and can trigger ischemic pain, ECG changes, and rhythm instability.
edges.target: Transient myocardial ischemia and electrical instability

edges.description: Reduced nitric oxide bioavailability permits excessive coronary smooth muscle constriction.

edges.source: Implantable cardioverter-defibrillator placement for high-risk arrhythmia
Implantable cardioverter-defibrillator placement for high-risk arrhythmia

nodes.description: Coronary adventitial inflammation contributes to Rho-kinase upregulation in vascular smooth muscle cells, priming coronary segments for abnormal hypercontractile responses.

evidence.snippet: This review summarises the landmark studies on the mechanisms of coronary vasospasm demonstrating the central role of Rho-kinase as a molecular switch of VSMC hypercontraction and the important role of coronary adventitial inflammation for Rho-kinase upregulation in VSMCs.

nodes.description: Coronary vascular smooth muscle cells undergo transient hypercontraction in epicardial and/or microvascular coronary segments. Rho-kinase signaling acts as a central molecular switch for VSMC hypercontraction.

evidence.reference_title: Coronary Spasm Testing with Acetylcholine: A Powerful Tool for a Personalized Therapy of Coronary Vasomotor Disorders.
evidence.snippet: Coronary vasomotor disorders (CVD) are characterized by transient hypercontraction of coronary vascular smooth muscle cells, leading to hypercontraction of epicardial and/or microvascular coronary circulation.

This review summarises the landmark studies on the mechanisms of coronary vasospasm demonstrating the central role of Rho-kinase as a molecular switch of VSMC hypercontraction and the important role of coronary adventitial inflammation for Rho-kinase upregulation in VSMCs.

nodes.description: Defective endothelial function reduces nitric oxide bioavailability and weakens vasodilator signaling, allowing acetylcholine or other stimuli to produce paradoxical constriction rather than normal coronary dilation.

evidence.reference_title: Recent insights into the mechanisms of vasospastic angina.
evidence.snippet: Patients with vasospastic angina are known to have defective endothelial function due to reduced nitric oxide bioavailability.

evidence.reference_title: Recent insights into the mechanisms of vasospastic angina.
evidence.snippet: Thus, endothelial dysfunction through abnormalities of eNOS and enhanced contractility of vascular smooth muscle in coronary artery segments are considered major mechanisms in vasospastic angina.

nodes.description: ICD placement is considered for selected patients with syncope or cardiac arrest from CAS-related tachyarrhythmia after weighing recurrence risk and response to vasodilator therapy.
nodes.id: Implantable cardioverter-defibrillator placement for high-risk arrhythmia

evidence.snippet: In patients with syncope or cardiac arrest caused by CAS-related tachyarrhythmias, or even bradyarrhythmias, implantation of an ICD or pacemaker, respectively, should be considered according to the risk of recurrence and efficacy of vasodilator therapy.

meta.association: Susceptibility allele for vasospastic angina in Japanese cohorts

evidence.reference_title: RNF213 Variants, Vasospastic Angina, and Risk of Fatal Myocardial Infarction.
evidence.snippet: The variants at the RNF213 locus showed the strongest association with VSA across the 3 datasets

evidence.reference_title: RNF213 Variants, Vasospastic Angina, and Risk of Fatal Myocardial Infarction.
evidence.snippet: Results of this study suggest that vascular cell dysfunction mediated by variants in the RNF213 locus may promote coronary vasospasm, and the presence of the risk allele could serve as a predictive factor for the prognosis.

nodes.description: Severe coronary spasm can precipitate sudden cardiac arrest, usually through malignant ventricular arrhythmia.

evidence.reference_title: Coronary artery spasm in cardiac arrest survivors.
evidence.snippet: Coronary artery spasm can be life-threatening.

evidence.reference_title: Coronary artery spasm in cardiac arrest survivors.
evidence.snippet: A high recurrence rate (up to 45%) of life-threatening ventricular arrhythmias was reported, and implantable cardioverter-defibrillator placement varied markedly.

nodes.description: Coronary spasm acutely decreases coronary perfusion and produces myocardial ischemia. Severe or prolonged ischemic episodes can provoke syncope, ventricular tachyarrhythmias, myocardial infarction, or sudden cardiac arrest.
nodes.id: Transient myocardial ischemia and electrical instability

evidence.reference_title: Vasospastic angina: a review on diagnostic approach and management.
evidence.snippet: Coronary artery spasm is a heterogeneous phenomenon that can occur in patients with non-obstructive coronary arteries and obstructive coronary artery disease, with transient spasm causing chest pain and persistent spasm potentially leading to acute myocardial infarction (MI).

evidence.reference_title: Life-threatening arrhythmias leading to syncope in patients with vasospastic angina.
evidence.snippet: Life-threatening arrhythmias are among the most serious complications of an ischemic attack caused by coronary spasm, and are associated with an increased risk of syncope and/or sudden cardiac death (SCD).

nodes.description: Ischemic attacks from coronary spasm can precipitate ventricular tachycardia or ventricular fibrillation.

evidence.reference_title: Variant angina and coronary artery spasm: the clinical spectrum, pathophysiology, and management.
evidence.snippet: Syncope is due to severe arrhythmias, including ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation, and high-degree atrioventricular block.

nodes.description: East Asian-specific rare deleterious RNF213 p.Arg4810Lys variant associated with vasospastic angina.
